---
title: "Knowledge-distillation random forest for shrub detection from drone imagery"
description: "A teacher-student machine learning model that detects target shrubs from drone RGB imagery alone, built to support restoration monitoring on the Carrizo Plain."
date: 2026-03-01
categories: [machine learning, remote sensing, R]
---

::: {.callout-note}
Course project (NR 418), winter 2026. Client: Sequoia Riverlands Trust. I led
the geospatial and machine learning work on a six-person team.
:::

## Overview

Restoration monitoring on mitigation lands in the Carrizo Plain region of
southeastern San Luis Obispo County. The goal was to detect a target shrub
species from low-cost drone imagery, so the client could track a restoration
plan without expensive multispectral sensors.

## Approach

The model uses knowledge distillation. A larger "teacher" random forest learns
from a rich set of engineered features: texture measures, color indices, and
principal components. That teacher then trains a lighter "student" model that
runs on RGB-derived features alone, with no extra sensor bands required. The
student keeps most of the teacher's accuracy while running on imagery any
consumer drone can collect.

## Methods

- Drone RGB imagery at sub-centimeter resolution
- GLCM texture features, vegetation indices, and PCA for the teacher model
- Random forest classification in R
- Repeated cross-validation for model selection
- A reduced feature set for the deployable student model

## Limitation and next step

RGB-only imagery was the main constraint. Adding a near-infrared band is the
clear next improvement for separating the target shrub from spectrally similar
vegetation.

<!-- [VERIFY] Vault sources conflict on the specifics: base/federal resumes say
target shrub, teacher 34 features / student 18 features, 94.6% teacher-student
agreement, 88% within-polygon accuracy. The IASNR CV frames this as "saltbush,"
a 29-layer mother model, ~96% accuracy. Reconcile species name and numbers
before adding hard figures to this public page. -->
